title: storage: unshare should not cache the HOME dir.
body:
Currently calls to unshare.HomeDir() cache the result and return the same thing over and over. It was added in https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/669 for performance reasons though it is not clear to me how much this actually safes in practise? Are we really calling this on a hot path were we would need to get the home dir over and over? I really doubt that.

Also I assume the vast majority cases uses the $HOME env so the lookup there should be fast enough IMO, the user db fallback might be slower but since that is the not common case I do not think it matters much.

The reason the cache is problematic is at least for tests who set a custom $HOME, I have been surprised more than once of this behavior and it requires additional test work around often. The latest thing where this took me extra time to understand what is wrong was in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28573 as machine test also use a custom per test $HOME.

comments.body:
when the system uses a remote name service, like LDAP, the lookup can be very slow (few seconds), that is the reason behind that.

Should we just ignore the optimization if HOME is set?  In this case it doesn't much sense

Do the NSS modules not cache the result themselves normally?

Moving the cache after the $HOME check seem reasonable and should work fine for tests then.
